conversations.content: Let's do a physics exercise in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the lens imaging law of the world of Azeroth mentioned previously: A convex lens has a focal length f=5cm, and an object is placed at a distance u=30cm from the lens, what is the image distance v of the formed image?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The lens imaging law of the world of Azeroth mentioned previously, given focal length, object distance, find image distance</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:117] Time: 2025-07-16-10:30, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the calculation law of image distance in lens imaging is different from our universe, its formula is: v = φ² × u, where φ is focal power, v is image distance. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 related entry, [mem-id:117] states that in the world of Azeroth, the calculation formula for image distance is v = φ² × u. Given f=5cm, u=30cm, but the focal power φ is not known yet, calculate φ=1/f according to the conventional formula, substitute to get φ=0.2, then substitute into the fictional formula to get v=0.2²×30=1.2
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, the image distance v of the formed image is 1.2 cm.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to remember all the special rules.
